what_it_does: This file documents the official contract for an experimental MCP channel capability that lets an MCP server push external events into a live Claude Code conversation as <channel> XML tags over stdio, and explains how to load development channels that bypass Anthropic's curated allowlist.
observations: The MCP server must declare capability experimental: { 'claude/channel': {} } for Claude Code to register a listener.
On an external event, the server emits mcp.notification({ method:'notifications/claude/channel', params:{ content, meta } }).
The notification lands in the conversation as <channel source='...' attr='...'>content</channel>.
meta keys become tag attributes; hyphenated keys are silently dropped; source is set to the server name.
The inject path is external system -> HTTP POST -> channel server handler -> mcp.notification() -> <channel> tag in the session.
--dangerously-load-development-channels server:<name> bypasses the curated allowlist after a confirm prompt, but the org channelsEnabled policy still applies.
standouts: stdio-only transport constraint for channel notifications.
External HTTP events are translated into in-conversation XML tag injection.
A deliberately named 'dangerous' dev flag bypasses the curated production allowlist.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
Markdown body enumerates a numbered contract.
Includes a part_of link to a parent board item.
concerns: The 'dangerously' flag implies elevated risk but the file gives no sandboxing or scope details.
Hyphenated meta keys are silently dropped without explaining why or warning the user.
No mention of content escaping, sanitization, or XML injection safeguards for the rendered <channel> tag.
The confirm prompt and org policy enforcement details are unspecified.
notes: This file is the reference contract for piping external channel events into Claude Code via an experimental MCP stdio notification and a development-only allowlist bypass.
questions: What is the exact schema and validation for content and meta?
How does the org channelsEnabled policy technically gate or log the dangerous dev flag?
What concurrency, ordering, or rate-limit behavior applies to incoming channel notifications?
How are special characters in content handled to avoid breaking the XML-like tag or the conversation context?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the mechanism/official contract, and the file matches that by detailing capability declaration, notification method, tag rendering, inject path, transport restriction, and dev bypass semantics.
apparent_themes: Security/allowlist model with org policy override
intention_signals: It aims to define a clean integration contract so third-party MCP servers can stream external notifications into Claude Code conversations, while keeping production use controlled by an allowlist and org policy, and development use possible via an explicit bypass.
novelty: It describes a mechanism that maps external HTTP events into in-conversation XML tags via MCP notifications, paired with a deliberately named dangerous development bypass of a curated channel allowlist.
contribution: Provides the canonical contract description for the experimental channel notification feature, including capability declaration, notification method, attribute mapping rules, stdio-only transport restriction, and development bypass semantics, which a fused system would keep as reference documentation.
summary_for_embedding: This noticeboard item documents the official contract for Claude Code's experimental MCP channel capability, where a server emits notifications/claude/channel over stdio to inject external events as <channel> tags with meta keys as attributes, and a --dangerously-load-development-channels flag bypasses the curated allowlist subject to org policy.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to specify how external systems can push channel notifications into a live Claude Code session and how developers can load non-allowlisted channel servers.
